name: release-please (Automated Release PRs)

auth.notes: GitHub token with repo permissions for creating PRs

performance.notes: GitHub API calls; depends on API rate limits

agent_readiness.notes: Automated release PR creation based on conventional commits. GitHub Action: uses: googleapis/release-please-action@v4 with: release-type: node. On merge of release PR → creates git tag + GitHub Release. release-please-config.json: { 'packages': { '.': { 'release-type': 'node', 'changelog-sections': [...] } } }. .release-please-manifest.json: version tracking. Supported release types: node, python, go, java, rust, etc. Parses conventional commits (feat!, fix, feat) to determine version bump (major/minor/patch). Automatically updates CHANGELOG.md. Creates release PR with version bump + changelog. Merge PR → tag created → package publish can trigger. Monorepo support. Works well with changesets as alternative.

gotchas: Requires strict conventional commits — no conventional commits = no releases
PR must be merged to trigger release — not CI-only
release-please-manifest.json must be committed and updated
best_when: GitHub-hosted projects using conventional commits wanting automated releases
Teams wanting release PRs as review gates before publishing
Node.js, Python, Go projects needing automated versioning + CHANGELOG
avoid_when: Teams not following conventional commits strictly
When changesets provides better monorepo release management
summary: release-please automates release PRs from conventional commits. GitHub Action: googleapis/release-please-action@v4. Parses feat/fix/feat! for minor/patch/major bumps. Auto-updates CHANGELOG.md. Creates release PR → merge → git tag + GitHub Release. Apache-2.0 by googleapis. GitHub-only. Strict conventional commits required. Complex monorepo config. Alternative to semantic-release with PR review gate before release.
